commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=af2216cbef96b0f1303b181c85c192670fead8a4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds QMD command-token helpers that bypass POSIX shell parsing for Windows drive-letter and UNC absolute paths and adds backend-config regression tests for those two path forms.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +31. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main routes memory.qmd.command through splitShellArgs, whose unquoted backslash handling strips Windows separators before QMD spawn resolution. I did not run a live Windows QMD repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor/maintainer follow-up for the path-with-spaces defect, Windows real behavior proof, and canonical selection against the other open same-root-cause PR.

target.title: fix(qmd): preserve Windows absolute paths in command resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #92302 — `memory.qmd.command` path mangled on Windows, making QMD memory backend unusable on Windows despite working CLI.

`splitShellArgs` treats `\` as a POSIX shell escape character. When the configured QMD command is a Windows absolute path (e.g., `C:\Users\...\qmd.js`), every backslash is consumed as an escape, stripping path separators and producing an unspawnable command.

This fix adds `isWindowsAbsoluteCommandPath()` to detect drive-letter (`C:\...`) and UNC (`\\server\...`) paths, and `extractQmdCommandToken()` to extract the command token by splitting on the first space instead of going through `splitShellArgs` for Windows paths.

## Changes

- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts` — add `isWindowsAbsoluteCommandPath()` and `extractQmdCommandToken()` helpers; use `extractQmdCommandToken` instead of inline `splitShellArgs` in `resolveMemoryBackendConfig`
- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts` — add regression tests for Windows drive-letter and UNC paths

## Verification

- All 28 tests pass (26 existing + 2 new Windows path tests)
- `pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts` ✓

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `splitShellArgs` consumes every `\` as a POSIX shell escape outside quotes, mangling Windows absolute paths into unspawnable strings.

**Real environment tested:** Node.js 22.22.0, Linux x64 (string parsing is platform-independent; the bug and fix are pure string logic).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Construct a Windows absolute path as configured in `openclaw.json`: `C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js`
2. Pass it through `splitShellArgs` directly (before fix)
3. Pass it through `extractQmdCommandToken` (after fix)
4. Verify the POSIX quoted path fallback still works

**Evidence after fix:**

```
=== BEFORE fix (splitShellArgs directly) ===
Input:  C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js
Output: C:UsersuserAppDataRoamingnpmnode_modules@tobiluqmddistcliqmd.js
Result: PATH MANGLED — backslashes stripped, unspawnable

=== AFTER fix (extractQmdCommandToken) ===
Input:  C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js
Output: C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js
Result: PATH PRESERVED — spawnable

=== POSIX path still works ===
Input:  "/Applications/QMD Tools/qmd" --flag
Output: /Applications/QMD Tools/qmd
```

**Observed result after fix:** Windows absolute paths (drive-letter and UNC) are preserved intact. POSIX quoted paths continue to work through `splitShellArgs` as before.

**What was not tested:** Real Windows spawn of `qmd.js` with this exact config — requires a Windows 11 machine with `@tobilu/qmd` installed. The original reporter's environment (`openclaw/openclaw#92302`) provides real-world Windows confirmation. The fix changes only string tokenization; the downstream spawn path through `resolveWindowsSpawnProgram` / `materializeWindowsSpawnProgram` is unchanged.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
